title: potentilla villosa subvar. obtusifolia
intro: Potentilla villosa subvar. obtusifolia, also known as rough-leaved cinquefoil, is a type of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Potentilla villosa subvar. obtusifolia include its ability to alleviate symptoms of menopause, such as hot flashes and night sweats, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to extract its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
